what_it_does: A standalone demo/card HTML page that showcases the generative core: it loads React UMD + the compiled DS bundle, defines one content tree spec and renders it through ContainmentTree at three LOD levels (summary/pitch/full) in scaled slide artboards, renders three diagram types (hub/pipeline/quadrant) through DiagramSolver, and provides an interactive network→hub morph button that re-renders the DiagramSolver with state before/after.
observations: Top comment is a @dsCard directive (group/name/subtitle/viewport) so the design-system gallery can pick it up as a card.
Loads React 18.3.1 + ReactDOM from unpkg via <script> with SRI integrity hashes, plus the local ../_ds_bundle.js.
Destructures { ContainmentTree, DiagramSolver } from window.ConceptVDesignSystem_c8f43c — a hashed bundle namespace.
Defines ONE spec (a 'Why teams switch to ConceptV' band with sections, clusters, zones, metric and bullet atoms with priority/detail) and renders it at all three LODs from the same object.
Scales a fixed 1100px artboard to each column via transform:scale and a ResizeObserver, locking frame height to scaled content.
Diagrams built with a small N(ids) helper; quadrant nodes carry x/y; edges are kind:'flow'.
Morph demo builds before-edges (all-pairs dependency) and after-edges (spokes from Vi) and toggles state on button click, re-rendering via a persisted React root.
All page CSS uses design tokens (var(--zone-ground), --accent-gold, --fs-*, etc.).
Inline copy in the spec restates the system's own thesis: 'One source of truth', 'Computed, not hand-set', 'Nothing drifts'.
standouts: Demonstrates the core thesis literally: the SAME spec object renders at three LOD levels with no per-level markup, and the morph shows positions re-computed from re-routed edges.
The artboard scale-to-fit with ResizeObserver lets a fixed-width slide design preview responsively inside narrow columns.
Self-referential content — the demo slide's bullets describe the very single-source/computed/no-drift properties the system embodies.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard gallery-card convention and the window.<NS> bundle consumption pattern.
Token-pure page styling (no raw colours/sizes except a fallback in body background).
Consumes the compiled bundle rather than the raw .jsx, matching the project's load-order rules.
concerns: Loads React from an external CDN (unpkg) — fine for a demo card but is an external dependency, not self-contained.
Hard-codes the hashed bundle namespace ConceptVDesignSystem_c8f43c, which will break if the bundle hash changes (a known stale-bundle hazard in this project).
notes: The live proof-of-concept card for the generative core — one spec rendered across LOD, three diagram types, and an interactive morph, all from the compiled bundle.
questions: Is the hashed namespace ConceptVDesignSystem_c8f43c stable across rebuilds, or does this file need regenerating when the bundle hash changes?
Does the gallery serve this with the bundle present, or could the CDN React load fail offline?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: subtitle says 'One content spec computed across LOD; diagrams computed from relationships' and the page does exactly that with one spec across LOD plus relational diagrams and a morph.
apparent_themes: the system demonstrating its own properties
intention_signals: Trying to make the generative core's claims visible and tangible — proving that one content tree yields summary/pitch/full automatically and that diagrams are positioned from relationships, including an animated network→hub morph as evidence nothing teleports.
novelty: A demo that doubles as an argument: it renders the identical spec across LOD side-by-side and animates a relational morph, so the page is both a gallery card and a falsifiable proof of the engine's behaviour.
contribution: Contributes the runnable demonstration/gallery card wiring that exercises ContainmentTree and DiagramSolver from the compiled bundle — the visible proof and example-of-use of the generative core.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML gallery card that renders one content spec across three LOD levels and three diagram types plus an interactive network→hub morph using the compiled ConceptV design-system bundle.
intention_for_embedding: To demonstrate live that one content spec is computed across LOD and that diagrams are positioned from relationships, proving the generative core's design=f(content,axis) thesis.
